_comment: The BUNDLED STARTER CHANNEL for Mainguard's dynamic agent CLIs (P2-22 §J-5).
This ships inside Mainguard.Agents as an embedded resource, so CLI selection works out of the box with
no hosted Mainguard channel: the user picks CLIs during OOBE (or later in settings), and each is
fetched from its pinned npm tarball URL, sha256-verified against this file, then installed INSIDE
the VM at /home/mainguard/mainguard/adapters (npm --prefix) and bind-mounted READ-ONLY into every agent
sandbox at /opt/mainguard/adapters. Nothing is baked into the agent image — installing a CLI later
reaches every new sandbox with no image rebuild.
PINS ARE LOAD-BEARING. Every version below is concrete (AdapterManifest.Parse REFUSES @latest/ranges),
and every sha256 is the hash of the exact tarball at payloadUrl — verified before any install runs, so
a breaking upstream release cannot change what a user gets. Versions/hashes captured + install-verified
2026-07-15 (AdapterStarterManifestTests asserts the schema; the hashes are re-verified by fetching).
To add/bump an adapter: fetch the tarball, `sha256sum` it, update version+sha256+payloadUrl+probe
substring together, and re-run the in-VM install matrix. Never bump a version without its hash.
{payload} in installCmd expands to the staged, hash-verified file — the install MUST consume the
bytes the pin covered rather than re-resolving from a registry (that would defeat the pin).
apiKeyEnvVar names the env var the CLI reads its model API key from; the spawn path injects the
user's key under exactly that name (audit fix #13 — a hardcoded ANTHROPIC_API_KEY meant codex never
saw its key). Omit it for CLIs that authenticate interactively (opencode is multi-provider and
configures its own credentials in-terminal), and no key is injected at all.
engines note: claude-code needs node>=22 and gemini-cli node>=20. The agent image ships node 20 via
Nix (/opt/toolchain), and the VM-side install host uses the payload's node. claude-code + codex +
opencode were verified running inside the hardened jail; gemini-cli is deliberately NOT in the
starter set — its bundle fails to parse on node 20 ('Invalid regular expression flags'), so it ships
once the toolchain moves to node 22.
